title: aira cespitosa var. media
intro: Aira cespitosa var. media, also known as variegated hair grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia and is characterized by its distinctive striped or variegated leaves. Aira cespitosa var. media has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of coughs and colds.
